activation_mode.mechanism: conditional_rules_paths: Cline supports path-conditional rules alongside always-on rules; conditions use glob patterns

auto_memory.mechanism: Cline does not have an AI-generated auto-memory system distinct from user-authored rules

cross_provider_recognition.mechanism: multi_source_rule_detection: Cline reads .clinerules/, .cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, and other provider rule files automatically

file_imports.mechanism: No file import/include syntax documented for Cline rule files

hierarchical_loading.mechanism: Cline rules are project-scoped or global; no per-subdirectory loading hierarchy documented

providerExtensions.name: Multi-Source Rule Detection
providerExtensions.description: Cline automatically detects and loads rule files from multiple formats beyond its own .clinerules/ directory: .cursorrules (Cursor), .windsurfrules (Windsurf), and AGENTS.md (agents.md standard). All detected types appear in the Rules panel with individual toggles. This means a project's existing rule files from other providers are automatically active in Cline without any migration step.

providerExtensions.name: Per-Rule Enable/Disable Toggle
providerExtensions.description: Every rule file has a toggle in the Rules panel to enable or disable it without deleting the file. This allows fine-grained control — e.g., disabling a strict testing rule during prototyping, or toggling a client-specific rule on/off per engagement — without modifying the rule file itself.

providerExtensions.name: Conditional Rules via YAML Frontmatter paths
providerExtensions.description: Rule files can include YAML frontmatter with a `paths` key (array of glob patterns). A rule only activates when the current context (open tabs, mentioned file paths, files Cline is editing) matches at least one pattern. Rules without frontmatter are always active. An empty `paths: []` permanently disables the rule. If frontmatter cannot be parsed, Cline fails open: the rule activates with raw content visible. Activation triggers a UI notification: "Conditional rules applied: workspace:<filename>".

providerExtensions.name: /newrule Slash Command for Interactive Rule Creation
providerExtensions.description: The /newrule slash command prompts Cline to guide the user through creating a new rule file interactively. Cline asks about communication style, coding standards, project context, and workflows, then saves the result to .clinerules/. This lowers the barrier to authoring well-structured rules.
